title: Stop the Line — risk escalation note
body:
STOP THE LINE — [matter] — [date/time]
Raised by: [name, role]
Headline risk: [one sentence — what is the material risk].
Facts: [the specific facts that evidence the risk — documents, clauses, dates].
Impact if unaddressed: operations [ ]; timeline [ ]; cost [ ]; legal/ethical [ ].
Decision needed: [the specific call required] — by [when] — from [who].
Recommended path: [option] because [reason]. Fallback: [option].
guidance: Kendry & Slate "Stop the Line" template — any team member may pause to flag a material risk provided they bring facts. Use for the Week-2 crisis and any time commercial pressure risks overriding a real issue. Keep it to one screen.

title: Plan on a page — legal tech initiative
body:
PLAN ON A PAGE — [initiative name]
Problem / opportunity: [what pain point this addresses; who feels it].
Users & personas: [primary users and their key need].
Proposed solution: [what it is]. Build / Borrow / Buy: [which, and why].
Scope: in [ ]; out [ ].
Success metrics: [2-3 measurable outcomes].
Cost & effort: [indicative].
Key risks & dependencies: [top 3].
Owner & next step: [name] — [next action, by when].
guidance: One-page canvas for a legal-technology initiative (Week 4). Pairs with the CLI "build, borrow or buy" and "one-page plan" readings. Seed example: automate the landlord-consent tracker for the Whitegum leases.
tags: legal tech;procurement;plan on a page;build borrow buy

title: Innovation one-pager
body:
INNOVATION ONE-PAGER — [idea]
Problem: [the problem worth solving].
Hypothesis: we believe [change] will produce [outcome] for [who].
Smallest pilot: [the cheapest test that would prove or disprove the hypothesis].
Success measure: [what result would justify scaling].
Scale / kill decision: [when we decide, and on what evidence].
Owner: [name].
guidance: Lightweight canvas for staging an innovation or change experiment (Week 9). Forces a testable hypothesis and an explicit kill/scale gate before investment.

title: Continuous-improvement retro
body:
BLAMELESS RETRO — [matter / phase] — [date]
What went well: [keep doing].
What did not go well: [facts, not blame].
Root causes: [why — go past the first answer].
What we will change: [specific, owned actions].
One experiment for next time: [a single change to test].
guidance: Blameless post-mortem template for the matter debrief milestone (Week 3 continuous improvement, Week 8 EX). Focus on process and system causes, not individuals.

title: Stakeholder communication styles — K&S / NexaCare
body:
COMMUNICATION STYLE GUIDE (case study)
Dr Alexandra Keller (CEO): commercial, decisive, outcome-first. Lead with the bottom line and the decision needed; keep it short.
Jonathan Wu (GC): disciplined, risk-weighted, "no surprises". Use headings; state the risk position, options and recommendation.
Elena Rossi (Head of HR): people-first, practical, plain language. Focus on staff impact, fairness and day-one readiness.
Daniel Okoye (CIO): precise, technical. Focus on operational continuity, cutover, data integrity and privacy.
Isabel Moreno (Corporate Development): analytical, impatient with ambiguity. Tie everything to the deal thesis and the numbers.
Thomas Richter (Property): detail-oriented, deadline-driven. Focus on consents, critical dates and continuity of clinic operations.
guidance: Reference for the persona-tuned stakeholder-update workflow (Week 2). Drafting agents reuse this to adapt tone per recipient.

title: Matter status report — structure
body:
MATTER STATUS REPORT — [matter] — [week ending]
Overall health: RED / AMBER / GREEN — [one line].
Progress this period: [key movements].
Conditions precedent: [tracker summary — satisfied / outstanding / at risk].
Upcoming deadlines: [next 5 business days].
Risks & decisions needed: [issue, owner, decision].
Budget: AI spend and effort vs the fixed-fee milestones — [on track / watch].
guidance: Weekly status-report structure for the matter (cross-cutting; Week 7 metrics, Week 4 measurement). Use with the operations status-report skill or as a drafting prompt.
